card.intuition: An in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, and message broker, offering specialized types beyond simple key-value pairs.
card.analogy: A giant, shared whiteboard where everyone can instantly write and read notes in specific formats like lists, sorted leaderboards, or set checklists.
card.time_complexity: O(1) for most operations (get/set); O(log N) for Sorted Sets
card.space_complexity: O(N) for elements stored in memory
when_to_use: Caching expensive database queries or API responses
Managing user sessions with fast access speeds
Implementing real-time leaderboards (Sorted Sets) or unique visitor counts (HyperLogLog)
gotchas: Redis is in-memory — if the server runs out of RAM, it will start evicting keys based on your policy (e.g., LRU), so it's not a place for 'permanent' storage unless configured with persistence
A long-running command (like 'KEYS *' on a large DB) will block all other requests since Redis is mostly single-threaded

exercise.prompt: Pick the correct Redis command for the specific use case.
hints: The 'R' in RPUSH stands for Right
exercise.test_code:
assert "SET" in redis_actions["Set a simple string value"]
assert "RPUSH" in redis_actions["Push to the end of a list"] or "LPUSH" in redis_actions.values()
assert "ZADD" in redis_actions["Add to a sorted set with a score"]
assert "INCR" in redis_actions["Increment a counter"]
print("PASS")
